assessments.rationale: Hot-water and chilled-water networks circulate treated water (direct match). Steam networks carry vapour on primary side (incompatible), but sensor is deployed on secondary water side or condensate return.
assessments.mitigation: Install sensor on secondary (hot-water) side of steam substation heat exchanger; Use for condensate return metering in steam substations; Focus go-to-market on hot-water and chilled-water networks (dominant segments)

assessments.rationale: 4th gen (50-70 degC) and chilled-water (4-15 degC) fully within 90 degC limit. 3rd gen supply (80-120 degC) may exceed limit, but return side (40-60 degC) is within spec. Gap at worst 1.3x (120/90).
assessments.mitigation: Target 4th generation low-temperature networks (50-70 degC supply, fastest-growing segment); Install on return pipe side of 3rd gen substations (40-60 degC return); Serve all district chilled-water cooling substations directly (4-15 degC)

assessments.rationale: Building substations operate at 2-6 bar after network pressure reduction. Differential pressure controllers maintain controlled conditions. Well within 10 bar limit.
Residential substation flow rates 5-40 l/min at DN20/DN25. Above minimum detection (4.2 l/min DN20). Substations modulate via control valve, not near-zero flow.
Closed, pressurised, chemically treated network. Water treatment with dosing and filtration maintains well below 2% air content.
Closed-loop, continuously pressurised network. Pressurisation units maintain static pressure. Partially filled pipe is a major failure event, not routine.
Building substations are compact but already accommodate inline energy meters per EN 1434. Standard substation design consideration, not a novel barrier.
assessments.mitigation: Specify sensor mounting position in substation piping design for 5-10x DN straight run; Use flow straightener vane upstream if space constrained (<$5/unit in volume); Coordinate with substation OEMs for integrated placement in standard packages

assessments.rationale: District substations operate at 4-90 degC across heating and cooling. Gradual seasonal/daily temperature changes. NTC compensation to ~0.2% adequate for energy calculation.
